title: Wholesale Account Manager
location: 057 King Street West, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
summary: Own and grow a portfolio of retail accounts across North America. You'll manage relationships, negotiate terms, and identify new business opportunities within the wholesale clothing market.
responsibilities: Manage and grow an assigned portfolio of wholesale retail accounts
Negotiate pricing, terms, and seasonal buying agreements
Prospect and onboard new B2B clients across Canada and the US
Prepare and present order forecasts and sales reports
Collaborate with the inventory team to align stock with client demand
Attend trade shows and industry events as a brand representative
requirements: 2+ years experience in B2B or wholesale sales
Strong negotiation and relationship management skills
Proficiency with CRM software (Salesforce or equivalent)
Excellent written and verbal communication in English
Ability to travel domestically on occasion
Fashion or apparel industry experience is an asset

title: Inventory & Buying Coordinator
location: 057 King Street West, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
summary: Oversee stock levels, coordinate with overseas suppliers, and support the buying team in sourcing seasonal collections. You'll be the link between supply and sell-through performance.
responsibilities: Monitor and manage inventory levels across all product categories
Liaise with overseas manufacturers and domestic suppliers
Support the buying team with seasonal order placement and tracking
Analyse sell-through data and flag slow-moving SKUs
Maintain accurate records in the ERP system
Coordinate inbound shipments and resolve delivery discrepancies
requirements: 2+ years in inventory management, buying, or merchandise planning
Experience with ERP or inventory management software
Strong analytical and Excel skills
Organised, detail-oriented, and comfortable with high SKU volumes
Knowledge of import/export processes is an asset
Fashion or wholesale industry background preferred

title: Outbound Logistics Supervisor
location: 057 King Street West, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
summary: Lead the outbound shipping team ensuring accurate, on-time dispatch of wholesale orders. You'll manage carrier relationships, oversee warehouse staff, and optimise fulfilment processes.
responsibilities: Supervise daily outbound shipping operations and warehouse staff
Manage relationships with freight carriers and third-party logistics providers
Ensure order accuracy and on-time dispatch for all wholesale accounts
Develop and improve standard operating procedures for fulfilment
Track KPIs including dispatch rates, error rates, and carrier performance
Handle escalations and resolve shipping discrepancies promptly
requirements: 3+ years in logistics, warehousing, or distribution operations
Previous supervisory or team lead experience required
Familiarity with WMS (Warehouse Management Systems)
Strong organisational and problem-solving skills
Forklift certification is an asset
Ability to work in a fast-paced physical environment

title: General Worker
location: 057 King Street West, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
summary: Support day-to-day warehouse operations including receiving stock, sorting garments, picking and packing wholesale orders, and maintaining accurate inventory records.
responsibilities: Receive, sort, and store incoming garment shipments
Pick and pack wholesale orders accurately according to packing slips
Maintain cleanliness and organisation of warehouse floor
Conduct routine stock counts and report discrepancies
Operate basic warehouse equipment safely
Follow all health, safety, and company policies at all times
requirements: No formal education requirement — training provided on the job
Ability to lift up to 25kg and stand for extended periods
Reliable, punctual, and able to work as part of a team
Basic English communication skills
Previous warehouse or retail stock experience is an asset
Must be legally entitled to work in Canada (LMIA pathway available)

title: Security Guard
location: 057 King Street West, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
summary: Maintain the safety and security of our warehouse and office facilities. You'll monitor access points, respond to incidents, and ensure a secure environment for staff, inventory, and visitors.
responsibilities: Monitor and control access to warehouse, loading docks, and office areas
Conduct regular foot patrols of the premises and perimeter
Operate CCTV systems and report suspicious activity
Respond promptly to alarms, incidents, and emergency situations
Complete accurate shift reports and incident documentation
Liaise with local law enforcement and emergency services when required
Enforce company security policies with staff and visitors
requirements: Valid Ontario Security Guard Licence (BST certified)
1+ year of security experience in a commercial or industrial setting
Strong observational skills and ability to remain calm under pressure
Solid English communication skills — verbal and written
First Aid / CPR certification is an asset
Must be legally entitled to work in Canada (LMIA pathway available)

title: Loss Prevention Officer
location: 057 King Street West, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
summary: Protect company inventory and assets by identifying and addressing theft, fraud, and shrinkage across our wholesale operation. You'll work closely with warehouse and logistics teams to enforce loss prevention protocols.
responsibilities: Monitor inventory movement to detect discrepancies, theft, or fraud
Review CCTV footage and conduct internal investigations
Collaborate with warehouse supervisors to identify shrinkage hotspots
Conduct employee awareness training on loss prevention procedures
Prepare detailed investigation reports and escalate findings to management
Coordinate with law enforcement on criminal matters when required
requirements: Valid Ontario Security Guard Licence
2+ years in loss prevention, asset protection, or a related security role
Experience with CCTV systems and inventory auditing
Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail
Discreet, professional, and capable of handling sensitive matters
Retail or wholesale industry experience is strongly preferred

title: Delivery Driver
location: 057 King Street West, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
summary: Deliver wholesale fashion orders to retail clients and distribution points across the GTA and surrounding regions. You'll represent APEX WHL on every route — punctual, professional, and reliable.
responsibilities: Load, transport, and deliver wholesale orders to client locations on schedule
Verify order accuracy and obtain proof of delivery at each stop
Maintain accurate trip logs, fuel records, and vehicle inspection reports
Handle returns, exchanges, and pickup pickups as directed by dispatch
Communicate delivery updates and exceptions to the logistics team
Keep vehicle clean, well-maintained, and compliant with safety standards
requirements: Valid Ontario Class G driver's licence with a clean abstract (max 2 minor violations)
1+ year of professional delivery or driving experience
Ability to lift up to 30kg and manoeuvre hand trucks / pallet jacks
Reliable, punctual, and professional in client-facing situations
Familiarity with GTA roads and surrounding areas
Must be legally entitled to work in Canada (LMIA pathway available)

title: Freight Driver (AZ / DZ)
location: 057 King Street West, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
summary: Transport large wholesale shipments between our distribution centre, supplier facilities, and major retail clients using Class AZ or DZ vehicles. A key role in keeping our supply chain moving efficiently.
responsibilities: Operate Class AZ or DZ vehicles for inter-city and regional freight runs
Safely load and secure cargo in compliance with transport regulations
Complete pre-trip and post-trip vehicle inspections per HTA requirements
Adhere to HOS (Hours of Service) regulations and maintain accurate logs
Communicate proactively with dispatch regarding delays or route changes
Handle bill of lading documentation and delivery confirmations accurately
requirements: Valid Ontario Class AZ or DZ driver's licence
Minimum 2 years of verifiable commercial driving experience
Clean driver's abstract (no major convictions in the last 3 years)
CVOR compliance and familiarity with transport regulations
Experience with air-ride trailers and reefer units is an asset
Must be legally entitled to work in Canada (LMIA pathway available)
